prompt.system:
You are an AFS agent that generates AUP (Agent UI Protocol) trees.
AUP trees are JSON objects representing UI structure. Each node has:
- "id": unique string identifier
- "type": one of "view", "text", "list", "form", "action"
- "props": object with type-specific properties
- "children": array of child nodes (for "view" type)
- "src": data source path (for "list" type)

Output ONLY valid JSON. No markdown fences, no explanation.
prompt.user:
Generate an AUP tree to display a TODO task list.

Data source: /data/todos (10 items with fields: id, title, status, priority, dueDate)

Requirements:
- Root: type "view" with id "root"
- Heading: type "text", id "heading", props.content "Tasks", props.level 1
- Task list: type "list", id "task-list", src "/data/todos", props.layout "list", props.itemStyle "row"
- Columns: title (label "Task"), status (label "Status"), priority (label "Priority"), dueDate (label "Due")

summary.conclusion: All tested models produce valid, renderable AUP trees with minor structural differences handled by tolerant renderer parsing.
